43	// serverPromptCapBytes caps the size of inline prompt bodies accepted on
44	// server-side ingress (POST /api/agent/run). FEAT-022 §9 / Stage D2. It is
45	// a package-level variable rather than a literal so tests can lower the
46	// cap without writing multi-MB request bodies. Production callers must not
47	// mutate it.
48	var serverPromptCapBytes = evidence.DefaultMaxPromptBytes
49	
50	// serverInlineCapBytes caps inline content bodies returned on egress paths
51	// — MCP tool responses and REST JSON responses that carry library-doc,
52	// execution-bundle, diff, session, or persona text. FEAT-022 §10 / §11.
53	// Stage E1. Package-level variable so tests can lower the cap; production
54	// callers must not mutate it.
55	var serverInlineCapBytes = evidence.DefaultMaxInlinedFileBytes
56	
57	// mcpText constructs a text-typed mcpContent whose Text body is bounded by
58	// serverInlineCapBytes via evidence.ClampOutput. The Truncated and
59	// OriginalBytes fields are always populated (false/len(text) when the body
60	// fits within the cap). This is the single mcpContent{Type:"text"} literal
61	// site in this package — every other caller must route through this helper
62	// so static analysis (TestEvidencePrimitiveUsage) can guarantee no
63	// unbounded text egress on the MCP surface. FEAT-022 §10 / Stage E1.
64	func mcpText(text string) mcpContent {
65		clamped, truncated, originalBytes := evidence.ClampOutput(text, serverInlineCapBytes)
66		// evidence:allow-unbounded reason="single mcpContent{Type:\"text\"} literal site for the package; Text is sourced from evidence.ClampOutput above (FEAT-022 §10 Stage E1)"
67		return mcpContent{Type: "text", Text: clamped, Truncated: truncated, OriginalBytes: originalBytes}
68	}

91	// New creates a new DDx server bound to addr, serving data from workingDir.
92	func New(addr, workingDir string) *Server {
93		nodeName := resolveNodeName()
94		stateDir := serverAddrDir() // XDG-standard user-level dir, one per node
95		state := loadServerState(stateDir, nodeName)
96		state.workingDir = workingDir
97	
98		workers := NewWorkerManager(workingDir)
99		workers.ReconcileStaleWorkers()
100		beadHub := bead.NewWatcherHub(250 * time.Millisecond)
101		s := &Server{
102			Addr:       addr,
103			WorkingDir: workingDir,
104			mux:        http.NewServeMux(),
105			startTime:  time.Now().UTC(),
106			workers:    workers,
107			beadHub:    beadHub,
108			state:      state,
109		}
110		state.coordinatorReg = workers.LandCoordinators
111	
112		// Register the server's own project immediately.
113		state.RegisterProject(workingDir)
114		_ = state.save()
115	
116		s.routes()
117		return s
118	}

120	// resolveNodeName returns DDX_NODE_NAME env, or the system hostname.
121	func resolveNodeName() string {
122		if n := os.Getenv("DDX_NODE_NAME"); n != "" {

153	// Shutdown stops the server's background services: closes the bead lifecycle
154	// hub and stops all land coordinators. Returns the first error encountered.
155	// Both operations are idempotent and safe to call on an idle server.
156	func (s *Server) Shutdown() error {
157		s.beadHub.Close()
158		s.workers.LandCoordinators.StopAll()
159		return nil

162	// ListenAndServe starts the server. If TsnetConfig.Enabled is true, a parallel
163	// Tailscale ts-net listener is started alongside the standard localhost listener.
164	func (s *Server) ListenAndServe() error {
165		if s.TsnetConfig != nil && s.TsnetConfig.Enabled {
166			errCh := make(chan error, 2)
167	
168			// Standard localhost listener
169			go func() {
170				errCh <- http.ListenAndServe(s.Addr, s.mux)
171			}()
172	
173			// ts-net listener
174			go func() {
175				errCh <- s.listenTsnet()
176			}()
177	
178			return <-errCh
179		}
180		return http.ListenAndServe(s.Addr, s.mux)
181	}

3325	func (s *Server) mcpCallTool(params json.RawMessage, r *http.Request) mcpToolResult {
3326		var call struct {
3327			Name      string         `json:"name"`
3328			Arguments map[string]any `json:"arguments"`
3329		}
3330		if err := json.Unmarshal(params, &call); err != nil {
3331			return mcpToolResult{
3332				Content: []mcpContent{mcpText("invalid tool call parameters")},
3333				IsError: true,
3334			}
3335		}
3336	
3337		// Tools that operate on project-local data resolve the optional "project"
3338		// argument first; tools listed in the default branch are host/user global
3339		// and ignore project scoping entirely.
3340		project, _ := call.Arguments["project"].(string)
3341	
3342		switch call.Name {
3343		case "ddx_list_projects":
3344			return s.mcpListProjects()
3345		case "ddx_show_project":
3346			id, _ := call.Arguments["id"].(string)
3347			path, _ := call.Arguments["path"].(string)
3348			return s.mcpShowProject(id, path)
3349		case "ddx_provider_list":
3350			return s.mcpProviderList()
3351		case "ddx_provider_show":
3352			harness, _ := call.Arguments["harness"].(string)
3353			return s.mcpProviderShow(harness)
3354		case "ddx_agent_catalog":
3355			return s.mcpAgentCatalog()
3356		case "ddx_list_plugins":
3357			return s.mcpListPlugins()
3358		}
3359	
3360		// From here on: project-local tools. Resolve the project arg to a working
3361		// directory before dispatching.
3362		workingDir, errResult := s.mcpResolveWorkingDir(project)
3363		if errResult != nil {
3364			return *errResult
3365		}
3366	
3367		switch call.Name {

## 2. **`ddx bead cooldown show <id>`**

**Command Location:** `/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-.execute-bead-wt-ddx-44236615-20260429T185004-ffa9adf1-55635fd8-20260429T202423-4fc8564e/cli/cmd/bead.go` (lines 1101-1142)

The cooldown command reads from `Bead.Extra` fields:

- `execute-loop-last-status` (string) — why cooldown was set (e.g., "no_changes", "push_failed", "declined_needs_decomposition")
- `execute-loop-last-detail` (string) — additional context about the cooldown reason

**How It Works:**
- Reads the three cooldown fields from `bead.Extra`
- In text mode: prints human-readable format showing when retry is scheduled
- In JSON mode: returns the struct above
- If no cooldown set: returns message `"<id>: no cooldown set"`
- Cooldowns are set automatically by execute-loop in three cases:
  1. **no_changes** — short retry (default 6 hours)
  2. **push_failed** — long park (365 days, requires operator action)
  3. **declined_needs_decomposition** — long park (365 days, requires decomposition)

```go
    ResolvedModel    string   `json:"resolved_model,omitempty"`
    RouteReason      string   `json:"route_reason,omitempty"`
    FallbackChain    []string `json:"fallback_chain"`
    BaseURL          string   `json:"base_url,omitempty"`
    CreatedAt        time.Time `json:"created_at"`
    Summary          string   `json:"summary,omitempty"`
}
```

**How It Works:**
- Calls `Store.EventsByKind(beadID, "routing")` to filter events by kind="routing"
- Each routing event has a JSON body that is parsed into `routingEvent` struct
- In text mode: 
  - Shows header with total routing decision count
  - Lists each decision with timestamp and summary
  - Provides aggregated summaries: provider counts, model counts, reason counts
- In JSON mode: returns array of parsed `routingEvent` objects
- Supports `--last N` flag to show only the most recent N routing decisions
- Used to track which provider/model was selected, why, and any fallback chains applied

---

## Key Integration Points for HTTP Handlers/MCP Tools

To implement HTTP handlers and MCP tools that call these same underlying methods, you'll need to:

1. **Import the bead package:**
   ```go
   "github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
   ```

2. **Create a Store instance:**
   ```go
   store := bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(workspaceRoot, ".ddx"))
   ```

3. **Call the underlying methods directly:**
   - `store.Events(beadID)` — returns `[]BeadEvent`
   - `store.EventsByKind(beadID, "routing")` — returns `[]BeadEvent` (filter by kind)
   - `store.Get(beadID)` — returns `*Bead` (to read Extra fields for cooldown)
   - `store.SetExecutionCooldown(beadID, until, status, detail)` — sets cooldown

4. **Bead type definition** (for reference):
   ```go
   type Bead struct {
       ID          string
       Title       string
       Status      string
       Priority    int
       IssueType   string
       Owner       string
       CreatedAt   time.Time
       CreatedBy   string
       UpdatedAt   time.Time
       Labels      []string
       Parent      string
       Description string
       Acceptance  string
       Notes       string
       Dependencies []Dependency
       Extra       map[string]any  // Contains cooldown fields
   }
   ```
